footer.tagline: Open-source, self-hosted, zero-knowledge encrypted fingerprint browser. Data encrypted locally, network behavior visible, fingerprint generated from controlled profiles.

hero.title: Trust nothing. Verify everything.
hero.subtitle: Veil is an open-source, self-hosted, zero-knowledge encrypted fingerprint browser. Data is encrypted locally, network behavior is visible, and fingerprints come from controlled profiles — for high-value multi-account operations, Web3, e-commerce, and automation teams.

features.title: Replace "trust the platform" with "verify the system"
features.description: Six core features that set Veil apart from other fingerprint browsers.

useCases.title: Built for high-value identity isolation
useCases.description: Multi-account, wallet isolation, storefront operations, automation teams — Veil turns "isolation" into a verifiable engineering concern.

roadmap.title: From local security to zero-knowledge collaboration

openSource.title: Make security promises visible, auditable, and rebuildable
openSource.body: Veil's core engine is AGPL-3.0 licensed. You can audit the code, build it yourself, self-host it, and verify every security claim we make.

finalCta.title: You don't need to trust us.
finalCta.body: You just need the ability to verify us. Download Veil and turn security promises into checkable engineering facts.
